title: Standartox stx_query() Data Dictionary
description: Reference schema for the data.table returned by standartox::stx_query(). Each row is one synthesized ecotoxicological endpoint from a study (e.g. an LC50, EC50, NOEC, LOEC) rather than raw measured data. Standardized from the US EPA ECOTOX knowledgebase. Column value counts and examples below were derived from an example query result ('stxDb', 922,476 rows); the top_values counts are indicative and will vary between queries.
row_grain: One synthesized study endpoint (result value) per row.
notes: Numeric columns 'concentration' and 'duration' use -9999 as a missing-value sentinel; exclude it before any calculation.
'concentration' contains physically impossible extreme maxima (up to ~4.6e31 g/l) that are unit-conversion artifacts and should be screened out before analysis.
'_orig' columns hold the originally reported value/unit before standardization; the non-orig columns hold the standardized value/unit.
'qualifier' and 'duration_unit' are constant in the example extract (single value each) and carry no information.
top_values shows the top 10 (or fewer) most frequent non-missing values for character columns, with their row counts.

columns.name: cas
columns.description: CAS Registry Number of the test substance. Primary clean key for identifying a chemical (no missing values in example).

columns.name: common_name
columns.description: Common / trivial name of the substance. Frequently missing (~6.6% NA in example).

columns.name: chem_name
columns.description: Formal / systematic chemical name of the substance.

columns.name: dtxsid
columns.description: EPA CompTox DSSTox substance identifier (DTXSID). Some rows missing.

columns.name: qualifier
columns.description: Relational qualifier for the reported concentration (e.g. '=', '<', '>'). Constant '=' in the example extract.

columns.name: concentration
columns.description: Standardized effect concentration value, expressed in the units of 'concentration_unit' (predominantly g/l). Uses -9999 as a missing sentinel and contains impossible extreme maxima (unit-conversion artifacts) that must be screened before use.

columns.name: concentration_unit
columns.description: Standardized unit of 'concentration'. Mostly mass/volume (g/l), but includes area- and organism-based units that are not directly comparable.

columns.name: concentration_orig
columns.description: Originally reported concentration value (as a string) prior to standardization.

columns.name: concentration_unit_orig
columns.description: Originally reported concentration unit prior to standardization (e.g. ug/L, mg/L, uM, application rates).

columns.name: concentration_type
columns.description: Dose basis of the concentration. Different bases (active ingredient vs formulation vs total vs dissolved) are not interchangeable and should not be averaged together.

columns.name: duration
columns.description: Standardized exposure duration in hours (duration_unit == 'h' throughout). Uses -9999 as a missing sentinel.

columns.name: duration_unit
columns.description: Standardized unit of 'duration'. Constant 'h' (hours) in the example extract.

columns.name: duration_orig
columns.description: Originally reported duration value (as a string) prior to standardization to hours.

columns.name: duration_unit_orig
columns.description: Originally reported duration unit prior to standardization (d = days, h = hours, wk = weeks, mo = months, mi = minutes, yr = years, hpf/dpf = hours/days post fertilization, dph = days post hatch, ht = ?).

columns.name: effect
columns.description: Broad category of the biological effect measured. Different effect categories represent different sensitivities and should not be pooled for an SSD.

columns.name: measurement
columns.description: Fine-grained description of what was measured within an effect category (high cardinality, ~7,000 distinct values).

columns.name: endpoint
columns.description: Specific endpoint statistic of the study result. Effect-based (e.g. LC50, EC50) and no/lowest-observed (NOEC/NOEL, LOEC/LOEL) endpoints must not be mixed within an SSD.

columns.name: endpoint_group
columns.description: Higher-level grouping of the endpoint. XX50 = median-effect endpoints (LC50/EC50/etc.), NOEX = no-observed-effect, LOEX = lowest-observed-effect, XX10/XX20/... = other effect percentiles, Bioconc = bioconcentration, XT50 = time-to-effect, MATC = maximum acceptable toxicant concentration. Some rows missing.

columns.name: exposure
columns.description: Exposure regime / route of administration. Aquatic water exposures (static, renewal, flow-through) should not be mixed with oral/dermal routes (gavage, dietary, spray). Some rows missing.

columns.name: organism_lifestage
columns.description: Life stage of the test organism at exposure. Heavily missing (~47% NA in example), often too sparse to use as a grouping factor.

columns.name: tax_group
columns.description: Broad taxonomic / trophic group of the test organism. Some entries are combined labels (e.g. 'algae || plant', 'macrophyte || plant'). Some rows missing.

columns.name: tax_taxon
columns.description: Binomial species name of the test organism. The natural aggregation key for 'one value per species' in SSD construction. Some rows missing.

columns.name: tax_genus
columns.description: Genus of the test organism.

columns.name: tax_family
columns.description: Family of the test organism.

columns.name: ref_title
columns.description: Title of the source study / reference. Useful for de-duplication and provenance, not as an SSD grouping key.
examples: Pesticide Ecotoxicity Database (Formerly: Environmental Effects Database (EEDB))

columns.name: ref_author
columns.description: Author(s) of the source study / reference.

columns.name: ref_year
columns.description: Publication year of the source study (stored as a string). Observed range 1915-2025.
